This indicates that the lower learning rate didn't help and may have hindered the model's ability to converge to a good solution. Analysis: So far, I've tried:
1. Different model capacities (256 vs 512 hidden units)

3. Regularization (dropout)
4. Different learning rates (1e-3 vs 1e-4)

The best result remains MSE 0.9882 with the linear model (256 hidden units, 2 layers) trained with a learning rate of 1e-3. Since I have only one iteration left, I should try to maximize my chances of improving the score by going back to the proven best configuration and perhaps training for more epochs without early stopping to see if I can squeeze out a bit more performance.

Decision: I'll train the best-performing model configuration (linear model with 256 hidden units, 2 layers, learning rate 1e-3) for more epochs without early stopping to see if longer training can improve performance. This is my last chance to potentially beat the current best score.
